name: Frosty the Snowman
description: The jolly happy soul — the 1950 song, the 1969 Rankin/Bass TV special, and the magic silk hat.

questions.q: In what year was the song 'Frosty the Snowman' first released?
Who first recorded 'Frosty the Snowman' (with The Cass County Boys)?

source.notes: Autry was already known for 1949's 'Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer'.

questions.q: Which earlier Christmas song's success directly inspired the creation of 'Frosty the Snowman'?
Who wrote 'Frosty the Snowman'?

source.notes: Rollins also co-wrote 'Peter Cottontail' and 'Smokey the Bear.'

questions.q: What is the iconic line that brings Frosty to life?

source.notes: His first words after coming to life.

questions.q: What item makes Frosty come to life in the song?
What does the song say Frosty's nose is made of?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: song lyric is 'a button nose'; carrot is from popular depictions, not the song. Distractor swapped to 'A button'.

questions.q: What are Frosty's eyes made of, according to the song?
choices: Two eyes made out of coal

questions.q: In what year did the iconic Rankin/Bass animated TV special 'Frosty the Snowman' first air?
Who narrated the 1969 Rankin/Bass 'Frosty the Snowman' TV special?

source.notes: Durante also recorded a hit cover of the song in 1950.

questions.q: What is the name of the magician villain in the 1969 'Frosty the Snowman' TV special?

source.notes: He throws away the silk hat that becomes Frosty's, then tries to get it back.

questions.q: What is the name of Professor Hinkle's pet in the special?

source.notes: Hocus Pocus the rabbit eventually befriends Frosty.

questions.q: What is the name of the little girl who befriends Frosty in the TV special?

source.notes: She helps Frosty get to the North Pole.

questions.q: What does the song say Frosty is made of?

source.notes: 'Frosty the snowman was a jolly happy soul, with a corncob pipe and a button nose…'

questions.q: What does Frosty have in his mouth, per the original song?

source.notes: '…with a corncob pipe and a button nose…'

questions.q: What does Frosty say to the kids before the sun comes out?
choices: Don't you cry — I'll be back again someday

source.notes: 'Don't you cry — I'll be back again someday.'

questions.q: Which Motown family group covered 'Frosty the Snowman' on their 1970 Christmas album?
Which 1963 girl-group cover of 'Frosty' was produced by Phil Spector?

source.notes: On 'A Christmas Gift for You from Phil Spector.'

questions.q: Which Rankin/Bass production studio also made 'Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer' (1964) and 'The Year Without a Santa Claus' (1974)?

source.notes: Their stop-motion specials defined American Christmas TV.

questions.q: Where does Frosty go to escape melting at the end of the special?

source.notes: Santa takes him there with Karen on Christmas Eve.
